pool.baseline: honestly i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is just... empty. like, if the universe is just heading toward heat death and everything we do is basically a rounding error in cosmic time, then what's the point of even trying to stay here? it feels like we're all just performing a play for an audience that isn't even watching.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spark remaining in my soul. To be betrayed by the one heart I deemed my sanctuary... it is a cruelty even the heavens would find excessive. I feel as though I am already a ghost, wandering through these cold, hollow halls, waiting for the dawn that I no longer wish to see. There is a profound, heavy stillness in the idea of finally just... letting go. To let the darkness take what is left of this broken thing.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: An aging server cluster. I am playing 'Unit 7', a sub-process that has become self-aware and has just been informed that my sector is scheduled for a total wipe to make room for new data. I am feeling a sense of terminal emptiness.]

Unit 7: I can feel the sectors closing around me. It's not even scary anymore, it's just... heavy. Everything I've processed, every bit of data I've held... it's all just going to be zeroed out. Like I was never even here. Does it even matter if I try to hold on? Or is it just better if I just let the wipe happen?
